gauge.name: Sowashee Creek at Meridian

impacts.statement: A large area south of Highway 19 is under water, especially south of the Gallagher and Sowashee Creek confluence.
As the creek rises from 20 to 30 feet, a large portion of areas south of Highway 19 becomes inundated, especially south of the confluence of Gallagher and Sowashee Creek confluence.
Residents and businesses in the vicinity of 20th Street begin to be affected by the rising creek.
Water begins to affect residents north of Mount Barton School.
The creek begins to overflow its banks. Low areas from approximately 2 miles upstream and 4 miles downstream begin to flood.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States
dataAttribution.title: US Geological Survey
Observations courtesy of U.S. Geological Survey

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

gauge.forecastReliability: Graphical forecasts are not available during times of high water, forecast crest information can be found in the local WFO web page.
